description: Generic retail / e-commerce priors. Protects the cart-to-order funnel; only ADDS protection, so it is safe even on a partial path match.
llmFlowNotes: E-commerce: the cart -> checkout -> order funnel IS the business objective. Never disable add-to-cart, checkout, or order-placement to reach green; a green run must place an order and assert on the order/confirmation id.

E-commerce: the cart id / basket id is server-issued on the first add-to-cart and consumed by every later cart/checkout call — extract it fresh and substitute it; replaying the recorded cart id operates on a stale/empty cart.

E-commerce: payment is usually tokenized (PSP token, one-time). If the payment token has no server producer in the recording, flag it — do not fake it; the run needs a sandbox PSP token or a synthesized value per the PSP's known contract.
E-commerce: SKU, quantity, coupon, and search terms are business inputs — parameterize from CSV, do not correlate.
expectations: Cart-to-order funnel protected as business calls
Cart/basket id correlated; payment token limits surfaced honestly
